reasoning: Chancroid is a bacterial STI caused by Haemophilus ducreyi. It is a neglected disease that has become increasingly rare due to effective antibiotic treatments, meaning there is extremely low commercial or public health motivation to develop a vaccine. Vaccine development takes many years of clinical trials, and there are currently no late-stage clinical trials for a Chancroid vaccine.
